title: ranunculus ficaria f. intermedius
intro: Ranunculus ficaria f. intermedius, also known as Lesser Celandine, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). Historically, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, which have been employed to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Lesser Celandine include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, or used in salves and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
